title: magnoliales
intro: The magnoliales rank is an order of flowering plants, characterized by a distinct arrangement of stamens and a three-lobed anther. This order includes a diverse range of families, such as the Orchidaceae, known for their showy and often fragrant flowers. The Euphorbiaceae, a family of plants commonly found in tropical and subtropical regions, also falls under the magnoliales order. Additionally, the Cyperaceae, a family of sedges and rushes, is another example of a family within this order.

families.family_desc: Annonaceae is a family of flowering plants commonly known as custard apple or soursop family, consisting of over 130 genera and more than 2,000 species.
Myristicaceae is a family of flowering plants commonly known as the nutmeg or myristica family, consisting of 100 species of evergreen trees and shrubs.
